summary: The diff implements REQ-2 and REQ-3 with reasonable coverage. REQ-2 is well addressed: MainApp.tsx uses useDoesCallerUserProfileExist as the authoritative check to skip the ProfileSetupModal for pre-existing users, and ProfileSetupModal.tsx adds a guard in handleSubmit to handle the race-condition case. REQ-3 is partially addressed on the frontend (guard in ProfileSetupModal), but the backend enforcement preventing non-admin re-registration is not clearly visible in the truncated diff. The AdminPanel conditional rendering constraint is preserved.

missing_requirements.requirement: Non-admin users cannot trigger a new profile registration if a profile already exists for their principal; attempting to register an already-existing user as a non-admin results in an appropriate user-facing message rather than a silent failure. Backend enforcement must exist so the frontend guard cannot be bypassed.
missing_requirements.reason: The frontend guard in ProfileSetupModal.tsx checks profileExists before submitting and shows a toast, which partially addresses this. However, the truncated backend main.mo does not show explicit enforcement that non-admin callers are rejected at the backend level when attempting to re-register an existing user. Without confirmed backend enforcement, the requirement is only partially satisfied.
